meaning: adj.被竭尽的,被取尽的,被汲尽的
examples: Chapter three is the empirical analysis of economic growth with exhaustible resources.
To conclude, energy is exhaustible , so we have to preserve it for our future generations.
People alive today will pass on a stock of exhaustible and other natural resources as well as machines, buildings and social institutions.
One is that America has given credible warning that its patience is exhaustible: the alternative to disarmament by inspection is disarmament by force.
